articles.full_text:
Skip to contentHyderabad sees rising temperatures amid Telangana heatwave
HYDERABAD: Summer conditions have intensified across Telangana, with maximum temperatures rising sharply and heat levels increasing across regions.
The heatwave began in the last week of March and reached peak intensity by the first week of April. Despite intermittent rainfall triggered by a surface circulation and trough over the Bay of Bengal, there has been little relief from daytime heat.
Heat persists despite light rainfall
Scattered light showers in the evenings have not eased humidity levels. Daytime conditions remain harsh, with hot winds and high temperatures affecting normal activity.
The Hyderabad Meteorological Department said temperatures are likely to rise further in the coming days. Most areas are recording high maximum temperatures, and residents are finding it difficult to step out even after 10 am.
Temperatures likely to reach 40°C
Officials warned that temperatures in Telangana could reach around 40°C. People have been advised to take precautions against heat exposure as hot winds continue and there is no significant drop in temperature.
Listen to Story

articles.title: Telangana Heatwave Alert: Temperatures Cross 41°C in Many Districts - Sakshi Post
articles.full_text:
Telangana: Temperatures Cross 41°C
Scorching heat has tightened its grip across Telangana, making daily life increasingly difficult for residents. From as early as 9 AM until 5 PM, intense sunlight and rising temperatures are forcing people to stay indoors and avoid outdoor activities.
According to the India Meteorological Department, dry weather conditions are expected to persist over the next week due to the influence of two trough systems. The weather department has warned that temperatures are likely to rise further in the coming days.
Temperatures Soar Across Districts
On Wednesday, more than 20 districts in the state recorded temperatures above 41°C, prompting officials to issue an orange alert for several regions. The department has also indicated that temperatures may increase by another 2 to 3 degrees Celsius over the next three days.
Some of the highest temperatures recorded include:
In Hyderabad, the temperature reached around 38°C, which is also above normal for this time of year.
Higher Than Last Year
Officials noted that temperatures recorded this season are significantly higher compared to the same period last year, indicating a stronger and earlier onset of summer conditions across the state.
Public Advisory
With the heatwave conditions intensifying, authorities have urged residents to take necessary precautions:
- Use protective gear like hats or umbrellas when outdoors
